text: The surgeon uses a da Vinci robotic system to perform a myomectomy on a six-centimeter intramural myoma. The left hand manipulates a tenaculum to hold the myoma, while the right hand uses monopolar scissors to cut through the muscle and serous layers. As the second muscle layer is incised, the fibroid becomes visible.

Although in that much deep myoma generally, the vasopressin doesn't give you the bettered plane of dissection and this is slowly separated and in during that surgery we have used one suprapubic port also in which suction irrigation will be used to help the sucking the blood out and blunt dissection of the layers of the myoma so davinci robotic surgery has a advanced technology in which the Instruments are articulating so that just like your finger, a human finger, you can go to the plane of the myoma and slowly you can dig it from inside the muscle layer. In laparoscopic instrument, the instruments are rigid and they have difficult articulation, manual articulation, but here the hand movement of the surgeon is translated inside the body of the patient. So that's why, three D vision and articulation gives you the good dissection.

The surgeon has removed the myoma and begins intracorporeal suturing using a 30 mm curved needle with number one white grill suture. The inner muscle layer is sutured first, with periodic suctioning of blood. The procedure is performed without a uterine manipulator to avoid uterine perforation. Continuous suturing is applied to the inner muscle layer, with surgeon's knots used for better approximation. The right side of the muscle is being approximated, and the same suture will be used for the outer layer once the inner layer is closed.

The surgeon begins suturing the outer muscle layer using the da Vinci robotic system, starting with a surgeon's knot followed by continuous suturing. The robotic articulation allows for precise needle rotation and deep suturing, enhancing the surgical technique compared to open or laparoscopic methods.

The surgeon completes the final layer of suturing using a da Vinci robotic system. A deeper bite is taken with the needle holder, which features integrated scissors for cutting the suture after each bite. The suturing is terminated with a surgeon's knot, effectively reducing bleeding.
